summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary MSG show – stellar second set highlighted by an epic Morning Dew and the Good Lovin→La Bamba→Good Lovin sequence.
review: The recording is a superb soundboard (the .sbd. tag and dozens of comments about crisp, full‑mix audio confirm this) with only minor quirks such as a missing opening chord on Shakedown and a trimmed Space. Musically the concert is a fan favorite: a short but solid first set gives way to a blistering second set where Watchtower, Terrapin, GDTRFB and especially Morning Dew soar, while the Good Lovin→La Bamba medley adds fun‑lovin energy.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80 reviews, majority 5‑star, unanimous praise for the performance and recording; only a handful of minor criticisms keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, full‑range mix with professional balance. Occasional issues: first few chords of Shakedown missing in some copies, Space segment slightly trimmed, rare pitch anomalies.

standout_songs: Good Lovin > La Bamba > Good Lovin
poor_songs: Shakedown (missing opening chords in some copies)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A concise, tight first set leads into an explosive second set that flows flawlessly from Shakedown through GDTRFB, Watchtower, Dew and the La Bamba medley; energy peaks on the post‑space jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for searing guitar work, vocal improvisations (especially the “I guess it doesn’t matter” line in Dew) and his charismatic La Bamba sing‑along; a few note that he hit an odd wrong note that added character.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass praised as rock‑solid and never lets down; also noted for backup vocals on Sugaree.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mixed feedback – some reviewers call his playing “slack” or “off”, while others defend him, noting the overall band tightness despite occasional slips.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (or Keith) praised for strong keyboard textures and backing vocals on Sugaree and other numbers; keyboards described as “wonderful” and integral to the jam flow.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drums and Space highlighted as intense, powerful, and a major driver of the second‑set energy.
